title: Protecting American Energy Production Act
plain_summary: This law stops the President from banning fracking across the whole country unless Congress agrees to it first. Fracking is a way to get oil and natural gas from underground by pumping water, sand, and chemicals into the ground under high pressure. The law says that state governments should be the ones making rules about fracking on state and private land, not the federal government. This keeps the President from making big decisions about fracking without getting permission from Congress first.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.
subjects: Presidents and presidential powers, Vice Presidents

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Protecting American Energy Production Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Protecting American Energy Production
sections.text: Congress believes that state governments should be the main ones in charge of making rules about hydraulic fracturing. Hydraulic fracturing is a process used to get oil and natural gas out of the ground on lands owned by states and private people. The President cannot stop or ban hydraulic fracturing across the country unless Congress passes a new law that gives the President that power. This rule applies even if other laws might say something different.
